1.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: glory (as very apparent), in a wide application (literal or figurative, objective or subjective)

something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
3.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,
